articles.title: CPI report cooler than expected; rate-cut odds rise
articles.summary: Headline CPI rose 0.2% month-over-month versus 0.3% expected. Fed funds futures now imply a 70% chance of a cut by July.

articles.title: Mortgage rates ease as 10-year Treasury slips below 4.30%
articles.summary: Bond traders bid up duration after softer retail sales data, dragging the 10-year yield lower and pulling 30-year mortgage quotes with it.

articles.title: Housing starts decline in April; permits revised lower
articles.summary: Builders pulled back as input costs and elevated mortgage rates weigh on new-construction demand.

articles.title: Powell signals data-dependent stance at Jackson Hole
articles.summary: The Fed chair reiterated that policy decisions will hinge on incoming inflation and labor data, leaving July open.

articles.title: MBS spreads tighten to post-2022 lows
articles.summary: Improved liquidity and lower implied volatility have compressed the option-adjusted spread on agency MBS to 135 bps.

articles.title: Bankrate survey: 30-year fixed unchanged at 6.91%
articles.summary: Lender quotes held steady as MBS spreads compressed slightly. Jumbo product priced inside conforming for the third straight session.

articles.title: Treasury auctions soft; long end backs up 6 bps
articles.summary: Tepid demand at the 30-year auction pushed long Treasury yields higher, threatening mortgage rate stability.

articles.title: Existing home sales tick higher as inventory rebuilds
articles.summary: NAR data showed a 1.8% rise in existing home sales, driven by Sunbelt markets and a slow recovery in listings.

articles.title: Fed minutes: officials wary of premature easing
articles.summary: Most participants saw rates as appropriately restrictive and emphasized patience until inflation shows sustained progress to 2%.

articles.title: Optimal Blue: lock volumes jump 12% week-over-week
articles.summary: Origination activity rebounded sharply as borrowers raced to lock in sub-7% quotes ahead of next week's PCE report.
